title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro officials propose turning Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve due to high repair costs.
The zoo requires about $57 million in repairs; city currently spends around $900,000 yearly for operation.
Zoo leaders and the public have mixed reactions; discussions continue with a transition team forming in July.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin Wong and Abigail Brown held a successful microwedding at Bow Market due to a blizzard cancellation.
FX series 'Love Story' highlights nostalgia for 1990s communication, featuring John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette.
Debate over using the term 'partner' prompts discussion, questioning if it's interchangeable with 'boyfriend' in relationships.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, 44, from Burlington, MA, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH to honor his late father.
Averback lost his father to glioblastoma in 2020 and overcame testicular cancer himself, reshaping his perspective on health and time.
Despite a severe blood infection, Averback resumed training, combining his marathon run with a personal tribute to endurance and survival.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots safety Kevin Byard is working out with rumored trade target, Eagles WR A.J. Brown.
Philadelphia declined a recent offer from New England and may wait until June to move A.J. Brown.
The Eagles reportedly want a first-round pick for Brown, as his trade value remains high given his performance.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens concluded his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
The Boston Bruins are considering adding Hagens to their roster after their quiet trade deadline.
Hagens, a top prospect, was the seventh pick in the 2025 NHL Draft and could bolster the Bruins' lineup.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Is This Thing On?' starring Will Arnett and Laura Dern, directed by Bradley Cooper, is streaming on Disney+.
The film 'Lone Wolf McQuade,' featuring Chuck Norris, is available on Tubi, Pluto TV, Plex, Prime Video, and YouTube.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' with Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan is streaming on Netflix.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora enters his eighth season as Red Sox manager, with his contract valid through 2027.
Cora is close to surpassing Terry Francona for second-most wins by a Red Sox manager, needing 134 victories.
Cora's future beyond 2027 is uncertain, with family priorities influencing his career decisions.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line of Viktor Arvidsson, Pavel Zacha, and Casey Mittelstadt has outscored opponents 35-16 this season.
The line's +19 goal differential is second in the NHL, only behind the Colorado Avalanche's top forward grouping.
Zacha, Arvidsson, and Mittelstadt have emerged as key contributors, each playing some of their best career hockey this season.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support MGH's pediatric cancer care.
Mahr first participated in the Boston Marathon in 2014, inspired by young patients and their courage at Heartbreak Hill.
Mahr emphasizes the need for more pediatric cancer research funding, running to honor patients and families at MGH.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Proposal to reduce Massachusetts income tax from 5% to 4% may appear on November ballot.
A Tufts University analysis estimates a $5.1 billion revenue loss and $1,250 annual savings for median households.
Governor Maura Healey opposes the tax cut, citing potential negative impacts on state-funded programs and competitiveness.
